quality.rationale: 基于地理投影定义的二维坐标变换推导，涉及线元匹配与导航背景，解答步骤完整清晰

origin.question: Problem 6.4. Mercator's projection is defined as follows: The map coordinates are rectangular Cartesian coordinates $(x, y)$ such that a straight line on the map is a line of constant compass bearing on the globe.(b) What is the metric of the globe in $(\mathbf{x},\mathbf{y})$ coordinates?
origin.solution: (b) Take the radius to be 1 for convenience. Then$$\begin{array}{l}\mathrm {d s}^{2} = \mathrm {d} \theta^{2} + \sin^{2} \theta \mathrm {d} \phi^{2} \\= \sin^{2} \theta (\mathrm {d x}^{2} + \mathrm {d y}^{2}) \\= \operatorname {s e c h}^{2} y \left(\mathrm {d} x^{2} + \mathrm {d} y^{2}\right). \tag {8} \\\end{array}$$
